input.headline: Nasa releases new images of interstellar comet 3I/ATLAS

raw.headline: Nasa releases new images of interstellar comet 3I/ATLAS

winner: Nasa released the new images and immediately got a cease-and-desist letter from the Interstellar Comet Owners' Association—turns out 3I/ATLAS was actually just a space condominium.

candidates.text: Why did the interstellar comet 3I/ATLAS refuse to go to NASA's party? Because it was *too out of this world*!
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 8.5, "reason": "Pun on 'out of this world' is clever and specific to the comet's interstellar nature, with good surprise factor and brevity."}

candidates.text: Nasa released a new image of Comet 3I/ATLAS—turns out, it wasn't a comet at all. It was just a very disappointed cloud that gave up on space.
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 8.0, "reason": "The joke is concise, surprising, and specific to the headline. It uses personification humor effectively with 'disappointed cloud,' which adds a whimsical twist."}

candidates.text: Why did NASA release those new images of Comet ATLAS? Because they wanted to show us that even space has *atlas*-size problems.
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 6.5, "reason": "The joke uses a pun on 'atlas' but is somewhat generic and lacks specificity about the comet or NASA's actions. The humor is mild and relies on a predictable wordplay."}

candidates.text: Nasa released the new images and immediately got a cease-and-desist letter from the Interstellar Comet Owners' Association—turns out 3I/ATLAS was actually just a space condominium.
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 8.5, "reason": "Creative twist with unexpected humor, good use of specificity and surprise"}

candidates.text: Nasa released new images of a comet and, while they're impressive, we're not sure if the comet was actually trying to send us a message or just really into astrophysics.
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 7.5, "reason": "The joke is concise, slightly surprising, and ties the comet's imagery to a humorous interpretation of intent. It's specific to the topic but could be more original or punchier."}
